Function Documentation

EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE const unsigned char * get_font_default_ptr (void )

Get the pointer to the embedded default font data

Returns

Pointer to font data

Definition at line 311 of file mirror.c.

311                                                 {
312   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set
313   int matrix_rain_val = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
314   log_info("[get_font_default_ptr] CALLED: matrix_rain=%d, g_font_matrix_resurrected=%p, g_font_default=%p",
315            matrix_rain_val, (void *)g_font_matrix_resurrected, (void *)g_font_default);
316   if (matrix_rain_val) {
317     log_info("[get_font_default_ptr] RETURNING matrix font");
318     return g_font_matrix_resurrected;
319   }
320   log_info("[get_font_default_ptr] RETURNING default font");
321   return g_font_default;
322 }




References g_font_default,
    g_font_matrix_resurrected, GET_OPTION, and
  log_info.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  unsigned int get_font_default_size (void )


Get the size of the embedded default font


Returns


Font data size in bytes


Definition at line 329 of file mirror.c.


329                                          {
330   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set
331   if (GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
332     return (unsigned int)g_font_matrix_resurrected_size;
333   }
334   return (unsigned int)g_font_default_size;
335 }






References g_font_default_size,
    g_font_matrix_resurrected_size, and GET_OPTION.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  void mirror_cleanup (void )


Definition at line 84 of file mirror.c.


84                           {
85   if (g_digital_rain) {
86     digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
87     g_digital_rain = NULL;
88   }
89   if (g_last_rain_output) {
90     SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
91     g_last_rain_output = NULL;
92   }
93   options_state_destroy();
94   platform_destroy();
95 }






References digital_rain_destroy(),
    options_state_destroy(), platform_destroy(), and

EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  char * mirror_convert_frame (uint8_t * rgba_data, int src_width, int
  src_height)


Definition at line 102 of file mirror.c.


102                                                                               {
103   if (!rgba_data || src_width <= 0 || src_height <= 0) {
104     return NULL;
105   }
106 
107   g_frame_count++;
108   // Derive a time value from frame count using actual target FPS from options
109   int target_fps = (int)GET_OPTION(fps);
110   if (target_fps <= 0) {
111     target_fps = 60; // Default to 60 FPS if not set
112   }
113   float time_seconds = (float)g_frame_count / (float)target_fps;
114 
115   // Get current settings from options or terminal detection
116   int dst_width = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
117   int dst_height = (int)terminal_get_effective_height();
118   color_filter_t filter = (color_filter_t)GET_OPTION(color_filter);
119   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
120   if (g_frame_count <= 3 || g_frame_count % 120 == 0) {
121     log_debug("frame=%u color_filter=%d color_mode=%d", g_frame_count, (int)filter, (int)color_mode);
122   }
123   if (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
124     color_mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR; // xterm.js supports truecolor
125   }
126   palette_type_t palette_type = (palette_type_t)GET_OPTION(palette_type);
127   bool preserve_aspect_ratio = true; // Preserve webcam aspect ratio
128   bool stretch = false;              // Don't stretch - maintain proportions
129 
130   // Build terminal capabilities structure with user's color mode
131   terminal_capabilities_t caps = {0}; // Zero-initialize all fields first
132   caps.color_level = color_mode;
133   caps.capabilities = 0;
134   caps.color_count = (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_NONE)  ? 0
135                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_16)  ? 16
136                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_256) ? 256
137                                                       : 16777216;
138   caps.utf8_support = true;
139   caps.detection_reliable = true;
140   caps.render_mode = (render_mode_t)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
141   caps.wants_background = (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND);
142   caps.wants_padding = true;
143   caps.palette_type = palette_type;
144   caps.desired_fps = 60;
145   caps.color_filter = filter;
146 
147   // Convert RGBA to RGB (strip alpha channel) and optionally flip horizontally
148   rgb_pixel_t *rgb_pixels = SAFE_MALLOC(src_width * src_height * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t), rgb_pixel_t *);
149   if (!rgb_pixels) {
150     return NULL;
151   }
152 
153   bool flip_x = GET_OPTION(flip_x);
154   bool flip_y = GET_OPTION(flip_y);
155   for (int y = 0; y < src_height; y++) {
156     for (int x = 0; x < src_width; x++) {
157       int src_x = flip_x ? (src_width - 1 - x) : x;
158       int src_y = flip_y ? (src_height - 1 - y) : y;
159       int src_idx = (src_y * src_width + src_x) * 4;
160       int dst_idx = y * src_width + x;
161 
162       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].r = rgba_data[src_idx + 0];
163       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].g = rgba_data[src_idx + 1];
164       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].b = rgba_data[src_idx + 2];
165       // Alpha (rgba_data[src_idx + 3]) is discarded
166     }
167   }
168 
169   // Apply color filter to pixels if needed (except rainbow - handled in ANSI stage)
170   if (filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE && filter != COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
171     // color_filter operates on packed RGB24 format
172     uint8_t *rgb24 = (uint8_t *)rgb_pixels;
173     int stride = src_width * 3;
174     apply_color_filter(rgb24, src_width, src_height, stride, filter, time_seconds);
175   }
176 
177   // Create image structure
178   image_t img = {.w = src_width, .h = src_height, .pixels = rgb_pixels, .alloc_method = IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD};
179 
180   // Get palette characters based on selected palette type
181   const char *palette_chars;
182   switch (palette_type) {
183   case PALETTE_BLOCKS:
184     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS;
185     break;
186   case PALETTE_DIGITAL:
187     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL;
188     break;
189   case PALETTE_MINIMAL:
190     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL;
191     break;
192   case PALETTE_COOL:
193     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_COOL;
194     break;
195   case PALETTE_CUSTOM:
196     // Custom palette uses user-provided characters from options
197     palette_chars = GET_OPTION(palette_custom);
198     if (!palette_chars || palette_chars[0] == '\0') {
199       palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD; // Fallback
200     }
201     break;
202   case PALETTE_STANDARD:
203   default:
204     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD;
205     break;
206   }
207 
208   // Convert to ASCII using capability-aware function
209   char *ascii_output = ascii_convert_with_capabilities(&img, dst_width, dst_height, &caps, preserve_aspect_ratio,
210                                                        stretch, palette_chars);
211 
212   // Clean up
213   SAFE_FREE(rgb_pixels);
214 
215   if (!ascii_output) {
216     log_error("ascii_convert_with_capabilities returned NULL");
217     return NULL;
218   }
219 
220   // Apply rainbow color filter to ANSI output by replacing RGB values
221   // This preserves character selection while applying rainbow colors
222   if (filter == COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
223     char *rainbow_output = rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(ascii_output, time_seconds);
224     if (rainbow_output) {
225       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
226       ascii_output = rainbow_output;
227     }
228   }
229 
230   bool matrix_rain = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
231   if (matrix_rain) {
232     // Initialize digital rain context if needed or dimensions changed
233     if (!g_digital_rain || g_digital_rain->num_columns != dst_width || g_digital_rain->num_rows != dst_height) {
234       if (g_digital_rain) {
235         digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
236       }
237       if (g_last_rain_output) {
238         SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
239         g_last_rain_output = NULL;
240       }
241       g_digital_rain = digital_rain_init(dst_width, dst_height);
242       if (!g_digital_rain) {
243         log_error("Failed to initialize digital rain effect");
244         return ascii_output; // Return without effect on error
245       }
246       g_last_rain_update_time = emscripten_get_now();
247     }
248 
249     // Update color from active filter (allows filter changes after initialization)
250     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(g_digital_rain, filter);
251 
252     // Time-based updates: only update effect every RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS
253     double current_time = emscripten_get_now();
254     double elapsed_ms = current_time - g_last_rain_update_time;
255 
256     if (elapsed_ms >= RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS) {
257       // Calculate actual delta time in seconds
258       float delta_time = (float)(elapsed_ms / 1000.0);
259       g_last_rain_update_time = current_time;
260 
261       // Apply effect and cache a copy
262       char *rain_output = digital_rain_apply(g_digital_rain, ascii_output, delta_time);
263       if (rain_output) {
264         // Free old cached output
265         if (g_last_rain_output) {
266           SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
267         }
268         // Cache a copy (we'll return the original rain_output)
269         g_last_rain_output = strdup(rain_output);
270         // Return the rain output (caller will free it)
271         SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
272         ascii_output = rain_output;
273       }
274     } else if (g_last_rain_output) {
275       // Use cached output
276       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
277       ascii_output = strdup(g_last_rain_output);
278     }
279   } else {
280     // Clean up digital rain context if it exists but effect is disabled
281     if (g_digital_rain) {
282       digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
283       g_digital_rain = NULL;
284     }
285     if (g_last_rain_output) {
286       SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
287       g_last_rain_output = NULL;
288     }
289     g_last_rain_update_time = 0.0;
290   }
291 
292   return ascii_output;
293 }






References apply_color_filter(),
    ascii_convert_with_capabilities(), rgb_pixel_t::b,
    terminal_capabilities_t::capabilities,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_count,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_filter, COLOR_FILTER_NONE,
    COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW, terminal_capabilities_t::color_level,
    terminal_capabilities_t::desired_fps,
    terminal_capabilities_t::detection_reliable,
    digital_rain_apply(), digital_rain_destroy(),
    digital_rain_init(), digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(),
    rgb_pixel_t::g, GET_OPTION, IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD,
    log_debug, log_error, PALETTE_BLOCKS,
    PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS, PALETTE_CHARS_COOL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL, PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD, PALETTE_COOL, PALETTE_CUSTOM,
    PALETTE_DIGITAL, PALETTE_MINIMAL, PALETTE_STANDARD,
    terminal_capabilities_t::palette_type, rgb_pixel_t::r,
    RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS, rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::render_mode, RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, TERM_COLOR_16,
    TERM_COLOR_256, TERM_COLOR_AUTO, TERM_COLOR_NONE,
    TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR, terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::utf8_support,
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_background, and
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_padding.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  void mirror_free_string (char * ptr)


Definition at line 296 of file mirror.c.


296                                    {
297   SAFE_FREE(ptr);

References SAFE_FREE.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  int mirror_init_with_args (const char * args_json)


Initialize mirror mode with command-line style arguments


Parameters


args_json JSON array of argument strings, e.g.
  ["mirror", "--width", "80",
  "--height", "40"]


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 46 of file mirror.c.


46                                                  {
47   // Initialize platform layer
48   asciichat_error_t err = platform_init();
49   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
50     return -1;
51   }
52 
53   // Initialize logging to stderr (console.error in browser)
54   log_init(NULL, LOG_DEBUG, true, false);
55   // Use debug-style format in WASM so browser console shows full log headers
56   log_set_format(OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG, false);
57 
58   // Parse space-separated arguments
59   char *args_copy = NULL;
60   char *argv[64] = {NULL};
61   int argc = wasm_parse_args(args_json, argv, 64, &args_copy);
62   if (argc < 0) {
63     return -1;
64   }
65 
66   // Initialize options (sets up RCU, defaults, etc.)
67   err = options_init(argc, argv);
68   free(args_copy);
69 
70   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
71     return -1;
72   }
73 
74   // Log option values after initialization
75   log_info("[mirror_init_with_args] After options_init: argc=%d, matrix_rain=%d", argc, GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
76 
77   // Initialize ANSI color code generation (dec3 cache for RGB values)
78   ansi_fast_init();
79 
80   return 0;
81 }






References ansi_fast_init(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    GET_OPTION, LOG_DEBUG, log_info, log_init(),
    log_set_format(), OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG,
    options_init(), platform_init(), and
  wasm_parse_args().
